title: Demonstrating the EasyAI ECO Suite - An AI-Powered Functional ECO Solution at DAC 2026 - Semiwiki
gist: Easy-Logic will showcase its EasyAI ECO Suite at DAC 2026, embedding AI engines across the full functional ECO flow (SYN, DFT, PR) via three engines (Auto Partition, Learning/I-Learning, Smart Caching) that CEO Dr. Xing Wei says deliver smaller patches, faster iterations, and more accurate timing in the final 'golden window' of chip design.

themes.theme: AI across the functional ECO workflow

insights.claim: Easy-Logic's EasyAI ECO Suite integrates AI engines across the entire functional ECO flow (SYN ECO, DFT ECO, PR ECO) to attack traditional bottlenecks in patch size, runtime, and timing accuracy rather than being a single-feature upgrade.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Positions ECO as another EDA sub-flow being disrupted by embedded AI; a competitive datapoint versus incumbent ECO tools.
insights.quote: EasyAI ECO Suite is not a single-feature upgrade,  it is a systematic integration of AI across the entire ECO workflow, delivering smaller patches, faster iterations, and more accurate timing.
insights.locator: Dr. Xing Wei, CEO of Easy-Logic, commented

insights.claim: Easy-Logic CEO Dr. Xing Wei frames ECO efficiency gains in the final 'golden window' of chip design as directly impacting time-to-market, arguing AI-EDA convergence gives designers true controllability over product schedules.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Schedule controllability is the pitched value; late-stage ECO speed is a lever on tapeout timing that buyers pay for.
insights.quote: In the final 'golden window' of chip design, every ECO efficiency gain directly impacts time-to-market.
insights.locator: Dr. Xing Wei, CEO of Easy-Logic, commented

themes.theme: Three intelligent engines and benchmark claims

insights.claim: Easy-Logic's Auto Partition engine uses AI to analyze circuit node correlations in an Analysis-Partitioning-Solve loop to break multimillion-gate ECO changes into manageable sub-problems, reducing patch size by over 20% on average and up to 90% in certain cases.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Quantified patch-size reduction is the headline benchmark to validate against competing ECO tools; smaller patches reduce risk and rework at tapeout.
insights.quote: Auto Partition reduces patch size by over 20% on average, and up to 90% in certain cases.

insights.claim: Easy-Logic's Learning/I-Learning engine analyzes structural and logic equivalences between old and new netlists to iteratively approach the optimal patch, reducing patch size by over 20% on average and exceeding 50% in some instances even on already-optimized netlists.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Claiming further gains on already-optimized netlists is a differentiation angle; verify with customer benchmarks.
insights.quote: patch size is reduced by over 20% on average, with some instances exceeding 50%

insights.claim: Easy-Logic's Smart Caching engine uses a Store-Reuse-Iterate cycle that deep-learns circuit structure on the first run and reuses results on subsequent runs, reducing runtime by up to 80% or more in multi-iteration ECO scenarios without parameter changes.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Since multiple ECO rounds are the norm, an 80% runtime cut in iterations is a meaningful schedule/cost benefit to benchmark.
insights.quote: runtime is reduced by up to 80% or more in multi-iteration scenarios
insights.locator: Smart Caching: Accelerating Multiple ECO Rounds
